sentences.text: Pippi vil gå på markedet i dag.
sentences.translation: Pippi wants to go to the market today.

words.grammar: et marked · markedet (the market) · markeder (markets) · marked can also mean 'marked/noticed'
ville (to want/will) · vil (present) · ville (past) · Jeg vil gerne = I would like to · modal verb

sentences.text: Hun tager en stor kurv med sig.
sentences.translation: She takes a large basket with her.

words.grammar: en kurv · kurven (the basket) · kurve (baskets) · also used idiomatically: få en kurv = to be rejected (romantically)
tage med (to take along) · tager ... med · tog med (past) · Må jeg tage det med? = May I take this?

sentences.text: På markedet er der mange farver og lyde.
sentences.translation: At the market there are many colours and sounds.

words.grammar: en farve · farven (the colour) · farver (colours) · hvad er din yndlingsfarve? = what is your favourite colour?
en lyd · lyden (the sound) · lyde (sounds) · høre en lyd = to hear a sound

sentences.text: Pippi køber æbler, brød og en stor hat.
sentences.translation: Pippi buys apples, bread and a large hat.

words.grammar: købe (to buy) · køber (present) · købte (past) · købt (past participle) · regular weak verb
et brød · brødet (the bread) · brød (loaves, pl.) · brødene (the loaves) · unchanged in plural

sentences.text: En mand siger: "Piger bærer ikke så store hatte!"
sentences.translation: A man says: 'Girls don't wear such large hats!'

words.grammar: bære (to carry/wear) · bærer (present) · bar (past) · båret (past participle) · strong verb
en hat · hatten (the hat) · hatte (hats) · hattene (the hats)

sentences.text: Pippi griner og siger: "Denne pige gør det!"
sentences.translation: Pippi laughs and says: 'This girl does!'

words.grammar: grine (to laugh/grin) · griner (present) · grinede (past) · grint (past participle) · regular weak verb
denne (common gender) · dette (neuter) · disse (plural) · demonstrative pronoun · denne her = this one here (emphatic)

sentences.translation: Everyone at the market laughs along.

words.grammar: alle (pl.) · al (common sg.) · alt (neuter sg.) · alle sammen = everyone together · alle andre = everyone else

sentences.translation: Pippi gives apples to the children.

words.grammar: give (to give) · giver (present) · gav (past) · givet (past participle) · strong verb · give nogen noget = to give someone something
et barn · barnet (the child) · børn (children) · børnene (the children) · irregular plural: barn – børn

sentences.text: De er glade og takker hende.
sentences.translation: They are happy and thank her.

words.grammar: glad (sg.) · glade (pl./definite) · meget glad = very happy · glad for noget = happy about something
takke (to thank) · takker (present) · takkede (past) · Tak! = Thank you! · mange tak = thank you very much

sentences.text: Pippi går hjem med den store hat på og en tom kurv.
sentences.translation: Pippi walks home with the large hat on and an empty basket.

words.grammar: tom (common) · tomt (neuter) · tomme (pl./definite) · en tom kurv = an empty basket · opposite: fuld (full)
hjem = home (motion toward) · hjemme = at home (location) · gå hjem = to go home · Jeg er hjemme = I am at home
